ArchitectureNotes.objective: Package portable OpenCode workflow plugins while keeping vvoc-owned config and memory data isolated from native OpenCode config.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not expand into a large monorepo or broad profile manager before plugin packaging, explicit memory, and vvoc config boundaries are stable.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Config file mutation must stay conservative because users may already have hand-maintained JSONC with comments.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: The file-based memory store does full scans and should stay explicit, simple, and easy to reason about until scale requires indexing.

contract.purpose: Resolve OpenCode and canonical vvoc config paths, edit JSONC safely, pin the current vvoc package version in OpenCode config, register managed OpenCode agents conservatively, patch provider baseURLs conservatively, render canonical vvoc.json, and inspect installation state.

export-resolvePaths.PURPOSE: Resolve OpenCode config paths plus the canonical vvoc.json path for the selected scope.

export-ensurePackageInstalled.PURPOSE: Ensure the npm package is registered in OpenCode config with a pinned package version.

export-writeProviderBaseUrl.PURPOSE: Write a conservative provider options.baseURL override into OpenCode config.

export-syncManagedAgentRegistrations.PURPOSE: Ensure OpenCode config contains the managed enhancer primary agent plus the bundled managed subagents.

export-syncVvocConfig.PURPOSE: Rewrite the canonical vvoc.json document while preserving valid current values.

target.source: src/lib/opencode.ts + src/lib/vvoc-config.ts + src/lib/agent-models.ts + src/lib/vvoc-paths.ts + schemas/vvoc/v1.json + schemas/vvoc/v2.json

notes.note-1: Main OpenCode config edits should preserve comments when only the plugin array changes.
notes.note-2: vvoc-owned settings now live only in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json.

contract.purpose: Expose init, install, sync, status, doctor, version, agent, preset, plugin list, config validate, path-provider, completion, and upgrade subcommands through citty while delegating managed enhancer/subagent registration to the helper layer.

export-init.PURPOSE: Bootstrap package registration and config scaffolding (interactive by default).

export-sync.PURPOSE: Refresh canonical vvoc.json plus managed prompt files.

export-doctor.PURPOSE: Diagnose installation drift or invalid config.

notes.note-1: Commands should stay thin and delegate file logic to helper utilities.

contract.purpose: Review OpenCode permission requests with a constrained Guardian agent and auto-allow only low-risk actions.

export-GuardianPlugin.PURPOSE: Register Guardian hooks, config agent, and permission review flow.

notes.note-1: Guardian now reads vvoc-managed config from `.vvoc/` or `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/` only.

contract.purpose: Persist explicit memory entries in the vvoc XDG data root, keep shared scope global across projects, support scope-aware CRUD, and rank lexical search results.

export-loadMemoryRuntimeConfig.PURPOSE: Load vvoc memory settings from global and project config files.

export-putMemory.PURPOSE: Create a scoped memory entry in the vvoc XDG data root.

export-searchMemories.PURPOSE: Return ranked lexical matches across relevant memory scopes.

notes.note-1: Memory remains explicit-only. The store must not inject entries into prompts or system context.
notes.note-2: `shared` scope is global across projects. Session, branch, and project scopes remain project-local.

contract.purpose: Add explicit memory tools to OpenCode, register a report-only `memory-reviewer` subagent, and inject a short system reminder that encourages proactive memory tool usage without loading stored entries.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with custom tools and config mutation

export-MemoryPlugin.PURPOSE: Register explicit memory tools and the `memory-reviewer` subagent.

notes.note-1: The reviewer is implemented as a bundled subagent, and the main chat path receives only a short memory policy reminder instead of prompt-injected memory entries.

contract.purpose: Inject reusable vvoc system guidance into primary chat sessions while skipping known subagents, using agent-aware message hooks instead of the global system transform hook.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with config-aware chat.message mutation

export-SystemContextInjectionPlugin.PURPOSE: Inject main-session guidance such as proactive explore usage into `message.system` for eligible primary agents.

notes.note-1: `experimental.chat.system.transform` lacks agent context, so this plugin uses `chat.message` to target primary sessions without polluting known subagents.

contract.purpose: List, show, and apply declarative named model-target presets stored in canonical vvoc.json without resetting targets that are not listed in the selected preset.

export-resolvePreset.PURPOSE: Validate a preset name against the current canonical vvoc.json preset map.

export-applyPreset.PURPOSE: Apply only the listed preset targets through existing vvoc and OpenCode write helpers.

contract.purpose: Validate the canonical vvoc.json configuration file with human-readable error reporting against the versioned vvoc JSON Schema.

param.name: stdout
param.type: terminal output with pass/fail status for vvoc.json

export-validateVvocConfig.PURPOSE: Parse vvoc.json and report schema violations.

contract.purpose: Display all installed OpenCode plugins with their status (enabled/disabled) and source paths.

export-listPlugins.PURPOSE: Scan OpenCode config and display plugin list with status.

contract.purpose: Apply global OpenCode provider patch presets that rewrite provider.options.baseURL conservatively.

param.name: WriteResult
param.type: created | updated | kept for global opencode.json/jsonc

export-resolvePathProviderPreset.PURPOSE: Map a supported preset like stepfun-ai to provider/baseURL config.

export-applyPathProviderPreset.PURPOSE: Resolve global OpenCode config paths and apply the preset.

notes.note-1: The command is global-only on purpose so the same endpoint patch can be applied consistently across machines.

contract.purpose: Auto-detect the current shell and install vvoc completion files idempotently for bash, zsh, and fish, including path-provider presets, preset names, and the `agent set|unset ` flow.

export-default.PURPOSE: Install shell completions for the detected shell.

export-detectShell.PURPOSE: Detect the active shell from environment variables.

contract.purpose: Interactive project initialization: registers @osovv/vv-opencode in OpenCode plugin array and scaffolds managed prompts plus canonical vvoc.json. Uses @clack/prompts for TTY prompts. Interactive mode is the default; --non-interactive flag enables batch mode.

notes.note-1: Interactive mode is the default. Pass --non-interactive for batch/scripted use.
notes.note-2: Uses @clack/prompts for cross-shell TTY prompts with Ctrl+C handling.

contract.purpose: Check npm for a newer vvoc release, install it globally with Bun, and trigger the default global sync flow from a fresh subprocess.

param.name: stdout
param.type: upgrade status, optional changelog, install progress, and sync results

export-runUpgradeFlow.PURPOSE: Execute the full global-only upgrade flow and warn instead of failing when post-install sync cannot complete.

export-fetchLatestVersion.PURPOSE: Query npm for the latest @osovv/vv-opencode version.

export-fetchChangelog.PURPOSE: Fetch and display best-effort release notes for the latest version.

DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-1: Resolve scope-specific OpenCode config paths plus the canonical vvoc.json path.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-2: Ensure `@osovv/vv-opencode@` exists in the OpenCode `plugin` array.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-3: Create a fully seeded canonical `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json` file.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.evidence: `vvoc status`, parsed config content, and successful typecheck/build.

DF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-3: Rewrite vvoc.json canonically while preserving current values.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.evidence: Stable rewritten `vvoc.json` and scope-correct managed prompt sync.

DF-PRESET-SWITCH.NAME: PresetSwitch
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-1: Resolve the selected scope plus the canonical vvoc.json path.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-2: Read configured presets and validate the requested preset name.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-3: Install managed prompts first if the preset touches vvoc-managed agents.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-4: Apply only the listed model-target overrides through the existing vvoc and OpenCode write helpers.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.evidence: Selected targets change, unlisted targets remain untouched, and CLI output prints a per-target summary.

DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianPermissionReview
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-1: Capture relevant tool and command intent for the session.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-2: Build a bounded transcript and planned action prompt.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-3: Run Guardian, interpret risk, and auto-reply only when below threshold.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.evidence: Guardian logs, toasts, and permission reply behavior inside OpenCode.

DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.NAME: MemoryAccess
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.step-0: Before model turns, the plugin appends a short system reminder that explicit memory tools exist, without injecting stored memory entries.
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.step-2: Resolve relevant scopes from session id, git branch, project, or shared key.
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.step-3: Read or mutate session, branch, and project entries under `$XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/projects//memory/`, and shared entries under `$XDG_DATA_HOME/vvoc/memory/shared//`.
DF-MEMORY-ACCESS.evidence: Correct file layout, deterministic CRUD/search behavior, and no automatic prompt injection.

DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.NAME: MemoryReview
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.step-1: Register the reviewer subagent through plugin config mutation.
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.step-2: The reviewer inspects memory with `memory_list`, `memory_get`, and `memory_search` only.
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.step-3: The reviewer returns a keep/update/merge/delete report without modifying entries.
DF-MEMORY-REVIEW.evidence: Bundled `memory-reviewer` agent with read-only memory tooling.

DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.NAME: SystemContextInjection
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-1: Build the known-subagent set from built-ins, plugin-managed agents, and config-defined subagents.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-2: Inspect the resolved agent name on each outgoing chat message.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-3: Append reusable vvoc system guidance only for eligible primary agents.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-4: Skip known subagents and internal helper agents so their prompts stay isolated.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.evidence: Primary agents receive one reusable guidance block; known subagents do not inherit it.

DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.NAME: SecretsRedaction
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.TRIGGER: provider-bound request or tool execution with secrets present
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-1: Load the secrets-redaction section from canonical vvoc.json with environment-variable substitution.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-2: Replace detected secrets in model-bound chat payloads with stable session placeholders.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-3: Restore placeholders in model completions and in tool arguments before local execution.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-4: Preserve session-local placeholder mappings with TTL and bounded cache behavior.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.evidence: Provider-bound payloads are redacted, while local tool execution and user-visible responses receive restored values where needed.

DF-PLUGIN-LIST.NAME: PluginList
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-1: Resolve OpenCode config path for the current scope.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-2: Parse plugin array and extract each plugin's source.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-3: Display plugin table with name, source, and enabled status.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.evidence: Correct plugin table when plugins are installed, and graceful empty state when none are configured.

DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.NAME: ConfigValidate
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-2: Parse the file with strict JSON rules.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-3: Validate required fields and types against the versioned vvoc JSON Schema.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-4: Report human-readable errors for vvoc.json or pass with OK status.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.evidence: Invalid JSON is caught and reported with line/column info; valid canonical configs pass silently.

DF-COMPLETION.NAME: ShellCompletion
DF-COMPLETION.step-1: Detect the current shell from environment variables.
DF-COMPLETION.step-2: Generate the shell-specific completion content for bash, zsh, or fish.
DF-COMPLETION.step-3: Write the completion file to the shell-specific location.
DF-COMPLETION.step-4: Patch shell init files idempotently when the shell needs an explicit loader.
DF-COMPLETION.evidence: Installed completion files match the real vvoc command tree; zsh installs a loader block that enables `compdef` before sourcing `_vvoc`.

DF-INIT.NAME: InteractiveInit
DF-INIT.step-1: Check --non-interactive flag. If absent and TTY detected, enter interactive mode with @clack/prompts.
DF-INIT.step-2: Prompt for scope selection (global/project) if interactive.
DF-INIT.step-3: Prompt for plugin selection to enable if interactive; accept --plugins flag in non-interactive mode.
DF-INIT.step-4: Write initial managed prompts plus canonical vvoc.json.
DF-INIT.evidence: Canonical config is valid JSON after init; Ctrl+C aborts cleanly; re-running init is idempotent; non-interactive mode works without TTY.

DF-UPGRADE.NAME: UpgradeCheck
DF-UPGRADE.step-1: Query npm registry for @osovv/vv-opencode dist-tags latest.
DF-UPGRADE.step-3: If newer, fetch best-effort changelog context and install `@osovv/vv-opencode@<latest>` globally with `bun add -g`.
DF-UPGRADE.step-4: After a successful install, launch `vvoc sync` in a fresh subprocess so the new package version and sync logic are used.
DF-UPGRADE.step-5: If sync cannot run or exits non-zero, keep the upgrade successful but warn the user to run `vvoc sync` manually.
DF-UPGRADE.evidence: Network failure is handled gracefully; already-latest case prints OK; install failures stop before sync; successful installs trigger the default global sync flow.

Phase-1.name: CLI Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Establish safe config mutation helpers and wire them into a usable Bun CLI.
Phase-1.step-1: Implement path resolution, JSONC OpenCode edits, canonical vvoc.json helpers, and installation inspection.
Phase-1.step-2: Expose install, sync, status, doctor, and Guardian config commands.

Phase-2.name: Guardian Packaging
Phase-2.goal: Move the existing standalone Guardian plugin into the repository and export it from the package.
Phase-2.step-1: Port Guardian and move runtime config to the canonical vvoc.json path.

Phase-3.name: Explicit Memory
Phase-3.goal: Add explicit persistent memory, store it in the vvoc XDG data root, and expose a report-only reviewer subagent.
Phase-3.step-1: Implement file-based scoped memory storage and lexical search.
Phase-3.step-2: Register memory tools and the `memory-reviewer` subagent via OpenCode plugin hooks.

Phase-4.name: CLI Enhancement Suite
Phase-4.goal: Add preset/configuration helpers and developer-experience commands to improve setup, inspection, and switching flows.
Phase-4.step-1: Validate canonical vvoc.json with human-readable schema errors.
Phase-4.step-2: List installed plugins with status, enabled/disabled, and source path.
Phase-4.step-3: Apply supported provider baseURL patch presets conservatively to the global OpenCode config.
Phase-4.step-4: List, inspect, and apply named model-target presets from canonical vvoc.json.
Phase-4.step-5: Install shell completions for bash, zsh, and fish with shell auto-detection.
Phase-4.step-6: Interactive project setup with prompts for config location and plugin selection.
Phase-4.step-7: Check npm for updates, install the global package with Bun, and run post-install sync from a fresh subprocess.

Phase-5.name: Session Guidance And Safety
Phase-5.goal: Inject reusable main-session guidance and keep provider-bound payloads free of raw secrets.
Phase-5.step-1: Append reusable vvoc system guidance only for eligible primary agents via `chat.message`.
Phase-5.step-2: Redact secrets before LLM requests and restore placeholders after completion and before tool execution.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only package source files, tests, and narrowly scoped documentation updates required by the approved module changes.
